description_long: This is a medium-sized, reddish-brown ceramic vase with a single, thick handle attached to its upper body. The surface is matte and slightly rough, showing visible signs of wear and restoration, including two areas with light-colored repair patches. Faint white chalk or paint markings, possibly catalog numbers or notes, are present on the body. The vase has a wide, rounded belly tapering to a narrower base and a slightly flared rim. The interior appears unglazed and matches the exterior in color. Its utilitarian yet historic appearance suggests it could be found in an urban park, garden, or as a decorative piece in a public square.
description: A reddish-brown ceramic vase with a single handle, visible repair patches, and faint white markings, showing signs of age and restoration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handle on the right, white markings, and a visible repair patch near the rim.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the thick handle, with the rim and body showing minimal markings.
description_view_2: Side view with the handle on the left, faint white markings, and a clear view of the vase's rounded shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting two prominent repair patches and no handle visible.
